summary.title: Kernel Product Interaction Broker Validation
summary.description: Validation of Kernel product interaction broker hardening features including commit SHA binding for production truth, evidence writer validation, policy context resolution, idempotency with payload hashing, contract schema hashing, and tenant/workspace isolation.

productInteractionBroker.purpose: Governed broker for product-to-product request/response interactions. Owns fail-closed request validation, contract version checks, policy evaluation, handler dispatch, timeout handling, idempotent replay, evidence persistence, and execution counters.

hardeningFeatures.description: Binds interactions to commit SHA for production truth. Production mode requires a valid 40-character hexadecimal Git SHA-1.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: Constructor validates commitSha field for production mode. commitSha() builder method sets the commit SHA. Validation checks presence and format.
validationRules: Production mode requires non-null, non-empty commit SHA
Commit SHA must match regex ^[a-fA-F0-9]{40}$
Non-production modes (DEVELOPMENT, TEST) do not require commit SHA
Commit SHA is stored in broker for potential evidence inclusion
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: KER-004 nested test class with 5 tests

hardeningFeatures.description: Requires real evidence writer in production mode. No-op evidence writer is rejected in production to ensure evidence persistence.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: Constructor validates evidenceWriter.isNoop() for PRODUCTION mode. DEVELOPMENT mode resolves omitted writer to local file-backed writer. TEST mode allows no-op for test convenience.
validationRules: Production mode requires real evidence writer (not no-op)
Development mode defaults to local file-backed evidence if no-op provided
Test mode allows no-op evidence writer
Evidence writer failures are converted to blocked outcomes
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: P0-01 nested test class with 3 tests

hardeningFeatures.description: Resolves policy context from trusted providers, not caller-supplied flags. Rejects caller-supplied authorization and consent flags to prevent privilege escalation.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: policyContextResolver.resolve() is called after contract validation. Replaces caller-supplied policy context with trusted context. Rejects forged 'authorized' and 'consentGranted' flags.
validationRules: Policy context is resolved from trusted provider after contract exists
Caller-supplied 'authorized' flag is rejected
Caller-supplied 'consentGranted' flag is rejected
Policy context includes tenantId, workspaceId, purpose, actor from trusted source
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: P0-02 nested test class with 2 tests

hardeningFeatures.description: Includes payload hash and contract metadata in replay key for idempotency. Uses canonical JSON hashing for deterministic payload hashing.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: interactionKey() includes payload hash, contract schema hash, contract version, provider ID, consumer ID, product unit ID. isSameInteractionIdDifferentPayload() detects idempotency conflicts.
validationRules: Replay key includes payload hash computed via CanonicalJsonHasher
Replay key includes contract schema hash
Different payloads create different replay keys
Same payload replays from cache
Same interaction ID with different payload is blocked as idempotency conflict
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: P0-03 nested test class with 2 tests

hardeningFeatures.description: Computes hash of contract schema for idempotency. Ensures contract changes invalidate cached interactions.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: computeContractSchemaHash() canonicalizes contract fields (contractId, contractVersion, providerProductId, consumerProductIds, requiresAuth, requiresTenant, requiresConsent, piiClassification, tenantScope, allowedCallerRoles, allowedPurposes, allowedLifecyclePhases, degradedModeAllowed) and hashes via CanonicalJsonHasher.
validationRules: Contract hash changes when allowed purposes change
Contract hash changes when lifecycle phases change
Contract hash changes when degraded mode changes
Contract hash is included in replay key for cache invalidation
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: Contract hash change tests with 3 test cases

hardeningFeatures.description: Enforces tenant and workspace isolation through policy context validation. Blocks interactions from wrong tenant or workspace.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: Policy context resolver validates tenantId and workspaceId against trusted context. Blocks if tenant or workspace mismatch detected.
validationRules: Blocks interaction from wrong tenant
Blocks interaction from wrong workspace
Blocks interaction with both wrong tenant and workspace
Allows interaction with correct tenant and workspace
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: SEC-001 nested test class with 4 tests

hardeningFeatures.description: Circuit breaker pattern for handling failures and preventing cascading failures.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: ProductInteractionCircuitBreaker integration. Optional circuit breaker can be configured via builder.
validationRules: Circuit breaker can be configured via builder
Circuit breaker is optional (defaults to built-in implementation)
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: Circuit breaker integration is part of broker architecture

hardeningFeatures.description: Monitors latency budgets and SLO violations. Tracks latency metrics per contract.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: latencyBudgetsByContract map stores latency budgets per contract. recordMetrics() checks SLO violation against latency budget. sloViolations counter tracks violations.
validationRules: Latency budget can be set per contract via builder
SLO violations are counted when latency exceeds budget
Latency metrics are recorded for all interactions

testCategories.name: Basic Functionality
testCategories.coverage: Request routing, tenant scope, policy denial, purpose requirement, evidence requirement, evidence writer failures, contract missing, contract hash changes, file evidence persistence, idempotency replay

testCategories.name: Performance
testCategories.coverage: Latency metrics within SLO, handler timeout

testCategories.name: P0-01: Evidence Writer Validation
testCategories.coverage: Production mode rejects no-op, test mode allows no-op, development mode defaults to file-backed

testCategories.name: P0-02: Policy Context Resolution
testCategories.coverage: Rejects caller-supplied authorized flag, rejects caller-supplied consent flag

testCategories.name: P0-03: Idempotency with Payload Hashing
testCategories.coverage: Different payload creates different replay key, same payload replays from cache

testCategories.name: SEC-001: Tenant/Workspace Isolation
testCategories.coverage: Blocks wrong tenant, blocks wrong workspace, blocks wrong both, allows correct scope

testCategories.name: KER-004: Commit SHA Binding
testCategories.coverage: Production mode requires commit SHA, production mode accepts valid commit SHA, rejects invalid commit SHA format, non-production mode does not require commit SHA, test mode does not require commit SHA

requirements: Commit SHA required for production truth binding
Commit SHA format validated (40 hexadecimal characters)

DEVELOPMENT.description: Development mode with local file-backed evidence
requirements: No-op evidence writer resolves to local file-backed writer
Relaxed policy validation for development convenience
Test policy resolver for test scenarios

metrics.metricCollection: Metrics are collected via MetricCollectorPort integration. Metrics include counters for request outcomes and timers for latency tracking.

policyEvaluator.purpose: Evaluates policy decisions for product interactions

evidenceWriter.purpose: Persists interaction evidence for audit trails

policyContextResolver.purpose: Resolves trusted policy context for interactions

contract.purpose: Defines contract schema for product interactions

productionTruthGuarantees: Production interactions are bound to specific commit SHA
Commit SHA format is validated (40-character hexadecimal)
Evidence writer is required in production (no no-op allowed)
Policy context is resolved from trusted providers only
Idempotency is enforced with payload hashing
Contract schema changes invalidate cached interactions
SLO violations are tracked and monitored
securityConsiderations: Commit SHA binding prevents deployment of unverified code
Evidence writer validation ensures audit trail persistence
Trusted policy context prevents privilege escalation
Caller-supplied flags are rejected to prevent forgery
Idempotency conflict detection prevents replay attacks
notes: Product interaction broker is hardened for production truth
Commit SHA binding is enforced for production mode only
Evidence writer validation prevents audit trail loss in production
Policy context resolution prevents privilege escalation
Idempotency with payload hashing ensures replay safety
Contract schema hashing ensures cache invalidation on contract changes
Test coverage includes all hardening features
Production truth is maintained through commit SHA and evidence writer validation
